title: Software Update - DFU - Headset at low battery - Software update tab in device settings
scenario: DFU should be blocked when headset battery is below 5% and not charging, and only allowed when the headset is plugged into power.
steps: 1. Set headset battery to less than 5% and ensure it is not plugged into power.
2. Navigate to Device Settings > Software Update tab and attempt to start the update.
3. Observe the system response when attempting to update at low battery.
4. While the popup is displayed, connect the charger to the headset.
5. Observe whether the popup changes or remains.
6. Plug the headset into power (charging) and attempt the update again.
7. Observe that the update is allowed to proceed.
8. During the update flow, unplug the headset from power.
9. Observe the system response after unplugging.
expected: The update should only proceed when the headset is charging. At low battery (less than 5%) and not plugged in, the update should be blocked with an "Update not available" popup. Connecting the charger while the popup is shown should not dismiss the popup. Unplugging during the update flow should block the update again.
